commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Protected maintainer-labeled PR with no narrow automated repair; maintainers need to review the warning-only scope and merge state.; Cleared: The diff only changes memory-core watcher warning logic and tests; it does not touch workflows, dependencies, lockfiles, credentials, install scripts, package scripts, or publishing surfaces. (sha=539f4f2e6839d8f1e87b5ddd898142924ba6247e)
commands.review_summary:
Adds one-shot startup watcher-pressure warnings for built-in memory and QMD watchers using Chokidar watched-entry counts and Linux directory watcher counts, with focused tests and timer cleanup.

PR surface: Source +100, Tests +105. Total +205 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR's changed warning behavior: mocked Chokidar watched maps over 2,000 entries and Linux directory watcher counts exercise the warning paths. I did not reproduce the original macOS FD storm in a live gateway during this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Protected maintainer-labeled PR with no narrow automated repair; maintainers need to review the warning-only scope and merge state.

target.title: fix(memory): warn on startup watcher pressure
target.body:
## Summary

- re-add a best-effort memory-watch FD pressure warning after #89246 reverted the earlier warning-only PR
- keep memory watching enabled by default and warn only when runtime watcher state is already large enough to be risky
- move the warning out of config validation so normal startup config loading does not recursively scan memory folders
- cover built-in memory watching and QMD collection watching with focused watcher tests

## How the warning works

- Built-in chokidar startup watchers still warn on chokidar `ready`, the deterministic signal that the initial scan completed.
- Built-in memory watching also schedules one startup pressure sample 10 seconds after watcher setup finishes, then stops.
- Linux native directory watching uses that startup sample to aggregate directory watcher pressure across watched roots.
- Runtime native-to-chokidar fallback still restores coverage. A newly-created fallback chokidar watcher warns on its own `ready`; extending an already-ready watcher is not continuously polled.
- The warning is one-shot per manager and does not continuously check watcher state.

## Related

- Replaces the warning behavior reverted by #89246.
- Follow-up to #89185, but now self-contained because #89185 was reverted.
- Refs #86613, where FD pressure was reproduced with large watched memory trees.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.watcher-config.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/validation.policy.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/config/validation.policy.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.watcher-config.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/qmd-manager.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager-sync-ops.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.ts extensions/memory-core/src/memory/manager.watcher-config.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
target.author: romneyda
